name: Boston Trifle
recipeIngredient: 7 tablespoons granulated sugar, plus 1 tablespoon granulated sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300 degrees and position the rack in the lower third.
2. Pour the cake flour and 7 tablespoons of granulated sugar into a sieve or sifter, then sift onto a sheet of waxed paper. Set aside.
3. Measure the additional 7 tablespoons plus 1 tablespoon of granulated sugar; set aside.
4. Separate the eggs: place whites in a heavy-duty mixer bowl and yolks in a 1 1/2 quart bowl.
5. Whisk the egg yolks with an electric hand mixer on high speed for 3 to 5 minutes.
6. Add vanilla extract, and continue whisking for 15 seconds until the mixture thickens, becomes pale yellow, and increases in volume. Test consistency by lifting a small amount of the mixture; if ribbons slowly dissolve back into the surface, proceed to whipping the whites.
7. Attach the bowl with egg whites to the mixer with the whisk attachment. Whip on medium-low speed for 30 to 45 seconds until bubbles form on the surface.
8. Stop the machine and sprinkle cream of tartar and remaining granulated sugar in the center; resume whipping at medium speed for 4 to 5 minutes until glossy and stiff but not dry or granular.
9. Detach the whisk bowl, tapping it against the side to free excess flour.
10. Pour egg yolk mixture onto whites while folding gently with a rubber spatula; avoid overmixing visible yolks.
11. Sprinkle half of sugar on top, then fold and repeat with remaining sugar.
12. Add sifted cake flour in thirds using rubber spatulas, incorporating just until combined after each addition.
13. Gently pour batter into an ungreased 9-inch springform pan; smooth the surface level.
14. Bake for 55 to 60 minutes until top is golden and lightly puffed.
15. Turn the spongecake upside down immediately post-baking, placing the edges of the pan on inverted glasses to maintain sponginess while cooling (cooling takes about 2 hours before turning right side up).
16. Release cake from springform pan: loosen crust around rim with a thin metal spatula, then tap and tilt pan until removable bottom detaches.
17. Assemble the trifle by splitting cake into three layers (~7/8-inch each), chilling custard filling in a bowl covered with plastic wrap to prevent creaming.
18. Whip heavy cream at Chantilly stage, fold into cooled custard, return to refrigerator.
19. Pour chocolate-butter glaze over chilled dessert; spread evenly using metal or rubber spatula.
20. Sprinkle chocolate flakes around the rim and chill for up to 1-2 hours before serving.
